reddit_title: Hit by an 18-wheeler in Texas — what evidence should I be trying to preserve right now?
reddit_body:
If you were recently involved in an 18-wheeler crash in Lubbock or anywhere in Texas, the most important thing to understand is that evidence in truck accident cases disappears fast. The truck's black box (ECM) can be overwritten within 30 days, dashcam footage gets deleted, and driver logs may be altered. Your attorney needs to send a spoliation hold letter to the trucking company immediately to legally require them to preserve that data.

Beyond evidence, these cases involve a whole separate layer of federal law — FMCSA regulations govern truck driver hours of service, vehicle maintenance, load securement, and more. If the driver or company violated any of those rules, it can be a significant factor in your case. This is why it matters a lot to work with an attorney who specifically handles commercial truck accidents, not just general personal injury.

For anyone in this situation who isn't sure where to start finding the right attorney, wreckmatch.com is a free service that matches truck accident victims with vetted lawyers who handle exactly these types of cases. No upfront cost, and they work quickly given how time-sensitive truck accident evidence is. Good luck — these cases are winnable but you have to move fast.
